phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Auto-Q the wave contact — your three-hit passive plus the boomerang's double pass bullies melee and mage alike at one.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: E unlocked: scout the swing angles now — every wall on the map is a gank escape or an entrance.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: Q-auto-passive chunks, W camouflage resets trades, and the swing turns jungle walls into highways.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Trade on every Q cooldown — the boomerang's return hit makes close-range trades double-dip.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Avengerang... no — R online: Vengeance executes the chipped from two screens. Chip, then channel.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: swing-autos shred. Force extended trades the passive shield math wins.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Akshan picks and avenges: swing the flank, execute the carry, and revive your team's mistakes... by avenging their killers.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — E-swing poke angles edge out the grounded-zone DPS when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your E-swing poke angles beat Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: Q-auto-passive chunks, W camouflage resets trades, and the swing turns jungle walls into highways.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight deficit through levels 4-5: Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS edge out your E-swing poke angles on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the grounded-zone DPS out-tick your E-swing poke angles in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.why: Full kit: Q-auto-passive chunks, W camouflage resets trades, and the swing turns jungle walls into highways.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Trade on every Q cooldown — the boomerang's return hit makes close-range trades double-dip.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Avengerang... no — R online: Vengeance executes the chipped from two screens. Chip, then channel.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: swing-autos shred. Force extended trades the passive shield math wins.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Akshan picks and avenges: swing the flank, execute the carry, and revive your team's mistakes... by avenging their killers.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — E-swing poke angles edge out the double-blade spirit dives when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Avengerang... no — R online: Vengeance executes the chipped from two screens. Chip, then channel.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Yone's double-blade spirit dives own this window — your E-swing poke angles don't answer them on first item yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Akshan picks and avenges: swing the flank, execute the carry, and revive your team's mistakes... by avenging their killers.
